title: Erysimum lasiocarpum
taxonomy: Erysimum lasiocarpum is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. This plant is commonly known as a wild mustard or a searocket, and it is native to the coastal regions of western North America. It belongs to the genus Erysimum, which comprises a group of flowering plants that are often referred to as wallflowers or rocket flowers. The family Brassicaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes many common garden vegetables, such as cabbage, broccoli, and cauliflower. Erysimum lasiocarpum is classified as a perennial herb, with a growth habit that is typically low to the ground.
